title: Running 7 Marathons on 7 Continents in 7 Days
subtitle: 7天7大洲7场马拉松

paragraphs.en: Michael Wardian pushed forward into the penetrating arctic wind, fighting the urge to speed up. Too much effort and he'd begin to sweat, which, he was told, would only increase the risk of hypothermia. At the 2014 North Pole Marathon, the temperature dipped to minus-22 degrees F, with a wind chill that made it feel even colder. Along the route, armed guards wandered the large sheets of floating ice to minimize the risk of polar bear attacks.
paragraphs.zh: 迈克尔·沃迪安在刺骨的北极风中奋力前进，克制着加速的冲动。用力过猛就会出汗，而他被告知这只会增加失温的风险。在2014年北极马拉松赛上，气温降至零下22华氏度，风寒效应让人感觉更加寒冷。沿途有武装警卫在巨大的浮冰上巡逻，以尽量减少北极熊袭击的风险。

paragraphs.en: I like to do stuff that scares me, Wardian said. With ice frozen to his beard, Wardian crossed the finish line that April afternoon in a winning time of 4 hours 7 minutes and 40 seconds, almost two hours slower than his personal best over 26.2 miles. The race for Wardian, however, was less about the result than overcoming his aversion to the cold.
paragraphs.zh: 沃迪安说：“我喜欢做让我害怕的事。”那个四月的下午，胡须上结着冰的沃迪安以4小时7分40秒的夺冠成绩冲过终点，比他在26.2英里项目上的个人最好成绩慢了近两个小时。然而对沃迪安来说，这场比赛的意义不在于结果，而在于克服对寒冷的厌恶。

paragraphs.en: In a few days, Wardian will once again compete in an unfamiliar territory and below-freezing temperatures. He will line up Monday in Antarctica for the first leg of the World Marathon Challenge — joining 32 other adventure seekers on an unusual journey where participants travel through different time zones and climates to run seven marathons on seven continents in seven days.
paragraphs.zh: 几天后，沃迪安将再次在陌生地域和冰点以下的气温中参赛。他将于周一在南极洲站上起跑线，开启世界马拉松挑战赛的第一站——与其他32名冒险爱好者一道，踏上一段穿越不同时区和气候、在七天内跑完七大洲七场马拉松的非凡旅程。

paragraphs.en: An elite ultra-runner, Wardian has his sights on breaking the event's record average marathon time of 3:32:25 set last January by U.S. Marine Corps captain Daniel Cartica. Wardian, a 42-year-old Arlington resident, is a record-breaking racer, known in the ultra-running community for seeking tough courses and setting world records. Last year, he ran 1,254.65 miles in 47 races.
paragraphs.zh: 作为精英超级马拉松选手，沃迪安的目标是打破美国海军陆战队上尉丹尼尔·卡蒂卡去年一月创下的3小时32分25秒的赛事平均马拉松时间纪录。42岁的阿灵顿居民沃迪安是一位屡破纪录的跑者，在超级跑圈以挑战艰难赛道和创造世界纪录而闻名。去年，他在47场比赛中跑了1254.65英里。

paragraphs.en: The World Marathon Challenge, like most of Wardian's running goals, will be about pushing his limits. I love diverse and unique challenges, he said. I'm definitely interested in seeing what I can handle and what my body can accept. That drives me.
paragraphs.zh: 和沃迪安的大多数跑步目标一样，世界马拉松挑战赛将关乎突破极限。他说：“我喜爱多样而独特的挑战。我绝对有兴趣看看自己能承受什么，身体能接受什么。这驱动着我。”

paragraphs.en: Something about the way Richard Donovan carried himself appealed to Wardian. Perhaps it was the sense of adventure Donovan displayed when they first met at the 2010 50K Championships in Galway, Ireland, where Donovan was the race director. The two hit it off, and soon Wardian was participating in Donovan's events. It was at the North Pole Marathon, a race that Donovan organizes, that Wardian first heard about the Irishman's plan for the World Marathon Challenge — a challenge that Donovan himself completed in 2009 and 2012.
paragraphs.zh: 理查德·多诺万的举止风范吸引了沃迪安。或许是2010年在爱尔兰戈尔韦50公里锦标赛上初次见面时，担任赛事总监的多诺万所展现的冒险精神。两人一见如故，沃迪安很快便开始参加多诺万的赛事。正是在多诺万组织的北极马拉松上，沃迪安第一次听说了这位爱尔兰人关于世界马拉松挑战赛的计划——多诺万本人曾于2009年和2012年完成该挑战。

paragraphs.en: I knew that many people had a goal of running seven marathons on seven continents during any time period, Donovan, 50, said. I felt the natural extension to this idea would be to try to achieve it within a seven-day period. Wardian started saving for the trip in 2014, connecting with sponsors and getting approval from his wife, Jennifer, before committing. Registration for the event costs 36,000 euros, which covers international charter flights to each of the seven marathon locations.
paragraphs.zh: 50岁的多诺万说：“我知道很多人的目标是在有生之年跑完七大洲的七场马拉松。我觉得这个想法自然的延伸就是尝试在七天内完成。”沃迪安从2014年开始为此行攒钱，联系赞助商并征得妻子詹妮弗的同意后才正式承诺。赛事报名费为36000欧元，包含前往七个马拉松举办地的国际包机费用。

paragraphs.en: The challenge is a test of both physical strength and mental fitness. Sleeping on a crammed plane, adjusting to different time zones and finding food to eat (Wardian is a vegetarian) would make it an exhausting trip over a month, let alone a week. The key to a race like this is getting comfortable being uncomfortable, said Becca Pizzi, last year's women's champion. The highs of the race are incredibly high, and the lows incredibly low.
paragraphs.zh: 这场挑战是对体能与心理素质的双重考验。睡在拥挤的飞机上，适应不同的时区，还要找东西吃（沃迪安是素食者），这即便在一个月内也是疲惫之旅，更不用说一周了。去年女子冠军贝卡·皮齐说：“这类比赛的关键是适应不适。比赛的高潮高得惊人，低谷也低得可怕。”

paragraphs.en: Since turning it into an organized event in 2015, Donovan has attracted a variety of runners. This year's challenge will feature a far more elite field, which includes Ryan Hall, America's fastest marathon runner. Despite his proven track record, Hall said he has no time goals and that he still suffers from the same fatigue issues that forced him to leave the professional ranks in 2015.
paragraphs.zh: 自2015年将其变成一项有组织的赛事以来，多诺万吸引了各类跑者。今年的挑战赛将迎来更精英的阵容，其中包括美国跑得最快的马拉松运动员瑞安·霍尔。尽管战绩斐然，霍尔表示自己没有时间目标，且仍受困于迫使他2015年退出职业赛场的疲劳问题。

paragraphs.en: Hall plans to run with his friend, Pastor Matthew Barnett of The Dream Center in Los Angeles — one of the six American men who will be competing. I don't expect to run a step with Mike, but I will be excited to see how he does, said the 34-year-old Hall, who began weight-lifting after retiring. If I finish within an hour of him in each marathon, I'd be surprised.
paragraphs.zh: 霍尔计划与他的朋友、洛杉矶梦想中心的马修·巴尼特牧师一同参赛，后者是六名参赛美国男子之一。34岁的霍尔退役后开始举重，他说：“我没指望能和迈克跑在一起，但我会很兴奋看他表现如何。如果每场马拉松我都能在他之后一小时内完赛，我会很惊讶。”

paragraphs.en: Instead, 43-year-old Petr Vabrousek, an elite Czech Ironman champion, is expected to be Wardian's closest challenger. To others on the trip, simply finishing will be its own reward. Sinead Kane of Ireland is aiming to become the first blind person to complete the challenge. And Beth Ann Telford, a 47-year-old federal government worker from Fairfax and the only American female in this year's mix, is using the event as a platform to raise money for cancer research. It's a cause with a personal connection to Telford, who was diagnosed with brain cancer in 2004.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，43岁的捷克铁人三项精英冠军彼得·瓦布鲁塞克预计将成为沃迪安最接近的挑战者。对旅程中的其他人而言，仅仅是完赛本身就是一种奖赏。爱尔兰的西尼德·凯恩立志成为首位完成该挑战的盲人。而来自费尔法克斯的47岁联邦政府雇员贝丝·安·特尔福德，作为今年唯一参赛的美国女性，正以此赛事为平台为癌症研究筹款。这项事业与特尔福德本人息息相关，她于2004年被诊断出患有脑癌。

paragraphs.en: Doing something like this is definitely the hardest challenge that I've ever done except for the chemotherapy and brain surgery, she said. It's going to raise awareness. I just wanted to do something that is epic and this certainly is right up there. Wardian, too, hopes his involvement will give him a platform to promote a cause. He recently became an ambassador for the United Nations Women's HeForShe initiative to fight inequalities faced by women and girls worldwide.
paragraphs.zh: 她说：“做这样的事绝对是我除了化疗和脑部手术之外做过的最艰巨的挑战。这将提高人们的意识。我只想做点史诗般的事，而这无疑堪称史诗。”沃迪安也希望自己的参与能为他提供一个推动事业的平台。他最近成为联合国妇女署HeForShe倡议的大使，致力于消除全球妇女和女童面临的不平等。

paragraphs.en: On a chilly December afternoon, Wardian wove through Washington's crowded sidewalks on the way home from his full-time job as an international ship broker. His elastic, 6-foot frame bounced gently and efficiently off the ground with each step of the hilly six-mile trip back to Arlington. This is a daily routine during the week for Wardian, who started racing professionally in 2003 and runs seven days a week, often multiple times a day. When he travels, he prefers to explore new places on his feet.
paragraphs.zh: 十二月一个寒冷的下午，沃迪安结束国际船舶经纪人的全职工作后，穿过华盛顿拥挤的人行道回家。他那富有弹性的六英尺身躯，在返回阿灵顿六英里起伏路途的每一步中，都轻盈而高效地从地面弹起。这是沃迪安工作日的日常，他自2003年开始职业参赛，每周跑七天，常一天跑多次。旅行时，他更喜欢用双脚探索新的地方。

paragraphs.en: But in some ways, Wardian still has trouble thinking of himself as a runner. For the majority of his childhood, Wardian devoted his energy to becoming a Division I lacrosse player — a dream he realized when he was recruited to play at Michigan State University. Once he decides to do something, he just works at it until he does it, Michael's younger sister, Mariele, said. Once he decides to do it, it's usually something that's going to happen. He's always been like that. He's a very motivated individual.
paragraphs.zh: 但在某些方面，沃迪安仍难以视自己为跑者。童年大部分时光，他将精力投入到成为一级长曲棍球运动员上——当他被密歇根州立大学录取时，这个梦想实现了。他的妹妹玛丽埃勒说：“一旦他决定做某事，就会一直努力直到做到。一旦他下了决心，那事通常就会成真。他一直都是这样，是个非常有动力的人。”

words.meaning: adj. 有动力的，积极主动的

paragraphs.en: It was only a year or so ago that Wardian realized that he had been a runner longer than a lacrosse player. It was not until he ran in the 2004 U.S. Olympic Marathon Trials — the first of three for Wardian — that he felt that he was a legitimate runner. Now more than 10 years and numerous ultramarathon national titles and world records later, he embraces that identity. Wardian wants to see how far his legs can take him, one epic challenge at a time.
paragraphs.zh: 直到大约一年前，沃迪安才意识到自己当跑者的时间已超过长曲棍球运动员。直到参加2004年美国奥运马拉松选拔赛——他三次选拔赛中的第一次——他才觉得自己是一名真正的跑者。如今十多年过去，在收获无数超级马拉松全国冠军和世界纪录之后，他欣然接受了这一身份。沃迪安想看看自己的双腿能带他走多远，一次一个史诗般的挑战。

paragraphs.en: I want to always keep doing things that are exciting, adventurous, different and most importantly, probably things I'm not the best at, Wardian said, because if you're not seeking things out that are challenging and difficult for you, then you're not growing. So I hope maybe people see what I do, and say, Okay, I want to do something different or try something new. I'm going to do something that scares me. That's what I'm hoping people will take from it.
paragraphs.zh: 沃迪安说：“我想一直做那些令人兴奋、冒险、与众不同的事，最重要的是，做那些可能我并不擅长的事。因为如果你不去寻找对自己有挑战、有困难的事，你就不会成长。所以我希望人们看到我的所作所为后会说，好吧，我想做点不一样的，或者尝试点新东西。我要去做让我害怕的事。这就是我希望人们能从中汲取的。”
